response.justification: This candidate is a web and full-stack engineer whose career centers on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang for web/mobile/GIS applications. The role demands native-level Japanese proficiency — a hard, non-negotiable requirement — which the candidate does not possess (listed languages: English, Romanian, German). Beyond the language barrier, the candidate lacks the core technical profile: deep C programming for embedded UNIX systems, Linux kernel investigation and modification, Layer-2/Layer-3 networking knowledge, and any security domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption, attack detection). A brief C/C++ stint at NI and a bachelor thesis using C++ do not constitute the embedded security firmware expertise this role requires.
strengths: Broad multi-language engineering background including some C/C++ and low-level work (NI LabWindows, embedded robotics, D-lang async I/O)
Demonstrated ability to work across the full stack and adapt to new technical contexts quickly
Experience with Linux environments and DevOps tooling (Docker, GitLab CI, deployment infrastructure)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is a stated hard requirement and an immediate disqualifier
No security domain knowledge: zero evidence of encryption protocols, 802.1X, MACsec, authentication systems, or embedded security features
No meaningful embedded networking or kernel experience; primary expertise is web/SaaS/full-stack, not LAN-switch firmware or Linux kernel modification

key_factors.factor: No security or embedded networking experience
